perturbation.original_text: 2.2 For the Term of this Agreement, Hydron shall be designated as a sponsor of the Dolphins in the Sunscreen/Skin Care Category (as defined below) and a licensee of the Marks (as defined below) in the Sunscreen/Skin Category by the Dolphins. For purposes of this Agreement, the term "Sunscreen/Skin Care Category" means the product category consisting of sunscreen, sun protection and similar skin care products.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 For the Term of this Agreement, Hydron shall be designated as a sponsor of the Dolphins and a licensee of the Marks by the Dolphins.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the definition of "Sunscreen/Skin Care Category," the contract becomes internally inconsistent because other sections, particularly Exhibit A regarding Sponsorship Rights, reference specific benefits tied to this product category. This omission introduces ambiguity and uncertainty in enforcement. The exact rights Hydron possess as a sponsor is no longer clear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXHIBIT A MIAMI DOLPHINS/HYDRON TECHNOLOGIES, INC. SPONSORSHIP RIGHTS Season-Long Merchandising PRINT ADVERTISING The Dolphins will provide to Hydron: o One full page, four (4) color advertisement for Hydron in all ten (10) issues of GameDay Magazine, the official publication of the National Football League and the Miami Dolphins distributed at Pro Player Stadium during each year during the Term. PROMOTIONAL MEDIA In connection with this Agreement, Dolphins will provide the following promotional media: 1996 BENEFITS PROVIDED TO HYDRON o Executive Suite: 320A. Use of the executive suite for the December 8, 1996 game vs. the New York Giants. The game will include twelve (12) tickets and two (2) parking passes. o Stadium Signage: Hydron sign will be in place on the West Scoreboard - Secondary Tri-Vision Panel for the December 8, 1996 game vs. the New York Giants and the December 16, 1996 game vs. the Buffalo Bills. o Product Sampling: Hydron will provide ten thousand (10,000) sunscreen samples which will be distributed to club level patrons prior to and during the December 8, 1996 game vs. the New York Giants. The Dolphins will permit Hydron to set up four (4) display booths which will be staffed by Hydron employees to distribute the sunscreen. 1997-2000 BENEFITS TO HYDRON o "Defensive Play of the Week" Promotion: Hydron will be the title sponsor of the 30-second Jumbtron video "Defensive Player of the Week" promotion which will be displayed during each Miami Dolphins home game. 9 o Product Sampling: The Dolphins will permit Hydron to distribute sunscreen product samples to fans in attendance at one (1) home game during each contract year. Hydron will set up four (4) display booths and will staff them with Hydron employees. The Dolphins will also permit Hydron to distribute sunscreen product samples during the first two (2) weeks of training camp at the Dolphins training facility in Davie during each contract year. o Team Affiliation: Hydron will be permitted to advertise itself as a "Proud Sponsor of the Miami Dolphins" in the skin care category. o Easements: The Miami Dolphins will use reasonable efforts to provide endorsements of Hydron sunscreen/skin protection products from its training staff.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the definition of 'Sunscreen/Skin Care Category', while Exhibit A explicitly details Hydron's sponsorship rights related to sunscreen products (e.g., product sampling, team affiliation in the skin care category, endorsements of sunscreen/skin protection products). By removing the definition, the scope of Hydron's sponsorship is ambiguous, creating a contradiction with the specifics outlined in Exhibit A. The removal creates a direct conflict with and makes it unclear how the other sections are to be enforced, thus creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7.2 Insurance. The Dolphins shall, at its own expense, maintain in effect throughout the term of this Agreement, comprehensive general liability insurance policies with carriers of recognized standing, with limits of liability of at least One Million Dollars ($1,000,000), governing any and all property damage and person injury (including death) arising out of activities covered by this Agreement. Hydron shall, at its own expense, maintain in effect throughout the term of this Agreement, comprehensive general liability insurance policies with carriers of recognized standing, with limits of liability of at least One Million Dollars ($1,000,000), covering any and all property damage and personal injury (including death) arising out of activities covered by this Agreement and shall obtain and maintain such additional insurance coverage as the Dolphins shall reasonably require with respect to any Sponsored Events or similar activities.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.2 Insurance. The Dolphins shall, at its own expense, maintain in effect throughout the term of this Agreement, comprehensive general liability insurance policies with carriers of recognized standing, with limits of liability of at least One Million Dollars ($1,000,000), governing any and all property damage and person injury (including death) arising out of activities covered by this Agreement. Hydron shall, at its own expense, maintain in effect throughout the term of this Agreement, comprehensive general liability insurance policies with carriers of recognized standing, with limits of liability of at least One Million Dollars ($1,000,000), covering any and all property damage and personal injury (including death) arising out of activities covered by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'and shall obtain and maintain such additional insurance coverage as the Dolphins shall reasonably require with respect to any Sponsored Events or similar activities', the contract creates an in-text contradiction. Earlier sections, especially Exhibit A, detail sponsored events. The modified section implies Hydron only needs $1,000,000 in coverage regardless of the event's risk, while the expectation, inferred from the sponsored events details, is that additional coverage might be necessary. This leads to uncertainty about the actual insurance requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXHIBIT A MIAMI DOLPHINS/HYDRON TECHNOLOGIES, INC. SPONSORSHIP RIGHTS Season-Long Merchandising PRINT ADVERTISING The Dolphins will provide to Hydron: o One full page, four (4) color advertisement for Hydron in all ten (10) issues of GameDay Magazine, the official publication of the National Football League and the Miami Dolphins distributed at Pro Player Stadium during each year during the Term. PROMOTIONAL MEDIA In connection with this Agreement, Dolphins will provide the following promotional media: 1996 BENEFITS PROVIDED TO HYDRON o Executive Suite: 320A. Use of the executive suite for the December 8, 1996 game vs. the New York Giants. The game will include twelve (12) tickets and two (2) parking passes. o Stadium Signage: Hydron sign will be in place on the West Scoreboard - Secondary Tri-Vision Panel for the December 8, 1996 game vs. the New York Giants and the December 16, 1996 game vs. the Buffalo Bills. o Product Sampling: Hydron will provide ten thousand (10,000) sunscreen samples which will be distributed to club level patrons prior to and during the December 8, 1996 game vs. the New York Giants. The Dolphins will permit Hydron to set up four (4) display booths which will be staffed by Hydron employees to distribute the sunscreen. 1997-2000 BENEFITS TO HYDRON o "Defensive Play of the Week" Promotion: Hydron will be the title sponsor of the 30-second Jumbtron video "Defensive Player of the Week" promotion which will be displayed during each Miami Dolphins home game. 9 o Product Sampling: The Dolphins will permit Hydron to distribute sunscreen product samples to fans in attendance at one (1) home game during each contract year. Hydron will set up four (4) display booths and will staff them with Hydron employees. The Dolphins will also permit Hydron to distribute sunscreen product samples during the first two (2) weeks of training camp at the Dolphins training facility in Davie during each contract year. o Team Affiliation: Hydron will be permitted to advertise itself as a "Proud Sponsor of the Miami Dolphins" in the skin care category. o Easements: The Miami Dolphins will use reasonable efforts to provide endorsements of Hydron sunscreen/skin protection products from its training staff.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 7.2 explicitly states that Hydron 'shall obtain and maintain such additional insurance coverage as the Dolphins shall reasonably require with respect to any Sponsored Events'. The changed text removes this requirement, implying Hydron only needs $1,000,000 in coverage. This directly contradicts Exhibit A, which details sponsored events like 'Product Sampling' and 'Defensive Play of the Week' that could reasonably necessitate additional insurance coverage due to potential liabilities. The removal creates a direct conflict regarding the required insurance coverage for sponsored events.

perturbation.original_text: 8.2 This Agreement may be terminated by the Dolphins or modified to reduce or eliminate certain promotional benefits (such as use of Marks or Sponsorship Rights), as described in Sections 2.3, 4.5 and 5.4 hereof. Upon any such termination or modification, the Dolphins will in good faith attempt to substitute a promotional benefit of equivalent promotional value for any benefits that the Dolphins was forced to eliminate; or, if the Dolphins is unable to substitute a promotional benefit of similar magnitude, then the Dolphins and Hydron shall attempt, in good faith, to agree upon an adjustment in the amount of fees payable by Hydron to the Dolphins under this Agreement. If the Dolphins and Hydron cannot agree upon an adjustment in the amount of fees payable hereunder, then Dolphins and Hydron agree to arbitrate the adjustment in fees and to be bound by the decision of the arbitrators. Any such arbitration shall be conducted in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.2 This Agreement may be terminated by the Dolphins or modified to reduce or eliminate certain promotional benefits (such as use of Marks or Sponsorship Rights), as described in Sections 2.3, 4.5 and 5.4 hereof. Upon any such termination or modification, the Dolphins will attempt to substitute a promotional benefit. If one or more events or benefits are unable to be rescheduled during the Term of this Agreement, the provisions of Section 8.2 shall apply in the same manner as if such failure to reschedule caused a termination of a Sponsorship Right.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the section about adjusting the fees through good faith negotiation or arbitration when benefits are eliminated, there is an internal contradiction. The original text provides recourse for the financial implications for the change of the benefits from Dolphins by either: substituting, negotiating a fees or lastly arbitrate, now with this section removed the new benefits or sponsorships that were given now may be of lower magnitude and monetary impact for Hydron, there is no action to be take, therefor is an in-text contradiction and not fair practice to Hydron. This change introduces ambiguity, and potential legal challenges, because there is no longer a mechanism outlined in the text about how any compensation will be given.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.2 This Agreement may be terminated by the Dolphins or modified to reduce or eliminate certain promotional benefits (such as use of Marks or Sponsorship Rights), as described in Sections 2.3, 4.5 and 5.4 hereof. Upon any such termination or modification, the Dolphins will attempt to substitute a promotional benefit. If one or more events or benefits are unable to be rescheduled during the Term of this Agreement, the provisions of Section 8.2 shall apply in the same manner as if such failure to reschedule caused a termination of a Sponsorship Right.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the recourse available to Hydron in the event that the promotional benefits are reduced or eliminated. The original text outlined a process for substitution of benefits, negotiation of fees, or arbitration, providing a mechanism for adjusting the financial impact. The changed text removes this mechanism, directly contradicting the implied right to compensation or adjustment for the reduction of benefits, as originally agreed upon. Because section 8.2 from the original text is also the contradicted text. This causes a strong in-text contradiction.
